entities.name: Robinhood Crypto
entities.summary: Official product surface for Robinhood Chain — the public face of the L2 and onchain crypto products.
entities.about:
Robinhood Chain is pitched as a permissionless Layer 2 for financial services and real-world assets, developed on Arbitrum infrastructure.

The brokerage app and the chain are different products: app = accounts, KYC, eligibility; chain = rails anyone can (in principle) deploy on.

Use official pages and docs as primary sources before secondary CT maps.
entities.risks: Official narrative ≠ security review of every third-party app on the chain.

entities.name: Robinhood Chain Docs
entities.summary: Primary developer documentation for building on Robinhood Chain.
entities.about:
Start here for network parameters, tooling, and builder-facing claims.

Prefer docs over CT threads when the two disagree.
entities.risks: Docs lag product launches; always check last-updated dates.

entities.name: Robinhood Wallet
entities.notes: Self-custody wallet surface; trending tokens; Pay checkout claims
entities.summary: Self-custody wallet surface that points users at Robinhood Chain activity (swaps, trending, checkout UX).
entities.about:
Wallet features (trending tokens, Apple/Google Pay checkout narratives, fee/gas promos) are product UX on top of the chain.

Time-box every promo claim — gas waivers and fee holidays expire.

Wallet inclusion of a token is not an endorsement of safety or liquidity.
entities.risks: Promo windows and trending lists change; verify terms and dates.

entities.name: Robinhood Earn
entities.notes: USDG lending via Morpho; ~7% APY narrative; insurance claims in newsroom
entities.summary: App-facing USDG lending product; underlying rails cited as Morpho with partner stack support.
entities.about:
Earn lets eligible users lend dollar-backed USDG via a self-custody path, with an official ~7% APY narrative and insurance claims in newsroom copy.

Infrastructure is powered by Morpho; partners named in launch narrative include Steakhouse, Ethena, Spark, and Maple.

This is an app product experience sitting on chain lending rails — not a guarantee of yield.
entities.risks: Yield is variable; eligibility and insurance terms are jurisdiction- and product-specific. NFA.

entities.name: Stock Tokens (official)
entities.summary: Official tokenized equities product on Robinhood Chain — the RWA thesis surface, not a memecoin market.
entities.about:
Stock Tokens are pitched as tokenized exposure to named equities/ETFs for eligible users outside restricted jurisdictions.

Official pages note they are not available in the US and are subject to restrictions elsewhere.

Listed onchain does not mean deep two-sided liquidity — listed ≠ liquid.

Use for product/mechanism understanding, not price targets.
entities.risks: Jurisdiction limits; brochure listings can fail at size; not direct share ownership in all legal senses — read official terms.

entities.name: Vlad Tenev
entities.summary: CEO / co-founder signal account for product direction (e.g. builder calls to embed stock tokens).
entities.about:
Useful as Tier-0 primary signal for strategy, not as a protocol docs substitute.

Landscape role: Company leadership signal. Official Robinhood product or leadership surface for the chain.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Social posts are not legal or product documentation.

entities.name: USDG / Paxos
entities.notes: Dollar-backed stable narrative; dominant stables mcap on chain
entities.summary: Dollar-backed stable narrative on Robinhood Chain; dominant share of stables mcap in early metrics.
entities.about:
USDG is central to Earn and settlement stories on RH Chain.

Stable dominance on a young chain can reflect product design and incentives, not permanent equilibrium.

Always separate stable float from DEX meme volume when reading landscape metrics.
entities.risks: Issuer, attestation, and redeemability details belong in primary issuer docs.

entities.name: Arbitrum
entities.notes: Orbit stack parent for RH Chain
entities.summary: Orbit / L2 stack parent for Robinhood Chain — security and architecture context.
entities.about:
RH Chain is developed on Arbitrum’s Layer 2 infrastructure with high-throughput / ~100ms block claims in official copy.

“Built on Arbitrum” is a stack claim, not a full safety sticker for every app, bridge, or sequencer operational choice.

Read Orbit/L2 docs for inheritance model, then map residual risks separately.
entities.risks: Stack inheritance ≠ app-level bridge/ops risk.

entities.name: Chainlink
entities.summary: Oracle and cross-chain messaging partner cited in day-1 / testnet ecosystem narratives.
entities.about:
Oracles and CCIP-class messaging are load-bearing for tokenized asset products and cross-domain flows.

Presence as a partner is infrastructure context, not proof that every RH app uses Chainlink correctly.
entities.risks: Wrong feed configuration is an app risk even when the oracle network is reputable.

entities.name: Alchemy
entities.summary: RPC / developer infrastructure partner for builders on Robinhood Chain.
entities.about:
Node and API infra makes the chain usable for apps; it is not a protocol risk model by itself.

Landscape role: RPC & developer infra. Infrastructure that helps builders and users access Robinhood Chain (RPC, oracles, bridges, compliance, security).

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Infra outages affect UX, not consensus guarantees.

entities.name: LayerZero
entities.summary: Omnichain messaging / bridge-layer partner cited for moving messages and assets across chains.
entities.about:
Cross-chain messaging expands product surface and attack surface at the same time.

Treat bridge and messaging paths as first-class risk when reading “ecosystem maps.”
entities.risks: Messaging ≠ removing bridge or solver risk.

entities.name: TRM Labs
entities.summary: Compliance / screening partner referenced in testnet and ecosystem narratives.
entities.about:
TRM-class tools support investigations and risk screening for institutions and platforms.

Presence signals compliance stack ambitions for a corporate L2.

Not a consumer trading app.
entities.risks: Compliance tooling ≠ user-level scam protection for every wallet action.

entities.name: BitGo
entities.notes: Custody partner cited in ecosystem coverage
entities.summary: Custody partner cited in broader RH Chain institutional coverage.
entities.about:
Custody rails matter for institutions bridging into onchain products.

Different from self-custody Wallet UX for retail.

Confirm product scope before assuming BitGo secures your personal keys.
entities.risks: Third-party custody terms and operational risk.

entities.name: Privy
entities.notes: Wallet/auth infra cited in eco maps
entities.summary: Embedded wallet / auth infrastructure cited in eco maps for RH builders.
entities.about:
Privy-style infra helps apps ship login and wallet UX without full custom key management.

Builder dependency, not an end-user yield product.
entities.risks: App integration quality varies; review the app that embeds it.

entities.name: QuickNode
entities.notes: RPC/node infra cited in eco maps
entities.summary: Node / RPC API infra for developers building on Robinhood Chain.
entities.about:
Reliable RPC is table stakes for wallets and dapps.

Outages affect UX, not L2 consensus guarantees.
entities.risks: Provider availability risk for apps that hard-depend on one endpoint.

entities.name: Blockscout
entities.summary: Block explorer surface for inspecting RH Chain transactions and contracts.
entities.about:
Explorers are primary tools for verification — contracts, txs, token holders.

Bookmark an explorer when researching any CA on RH.
entities.risks: UI phishing clones exist industry-wide — verify URL.

entities.name: Allium
entities.summary: Onchain data infra
entities.about:
Data infra partner (testnet announcement)

Landscape role: Onchain data infra. Infrastructure that helps builders and users access Robinhood Chain (RPC, oracles, bridges, compliance, security).

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Infra presence improves UX; it does not remove protocol or bridge risk for end users.

entities.name: Fireblocks
entities.notes: Institutional controls cited in eco maps
entities.summary: Institutional wallet infra
entities.about:
Institutional controls cited in eco maps

Landscape role: Institutional wallet infra. Infrastructure that helps builders and users access Robinhood Chain (RPC, oracles, bridges, compliance, security).

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Infra presence improves UX; it does not remove protocol or bridge risk for end users.

entities.name: Chainalysis
entities.notes: Compliance tooling cited in eco maps
entities.summary: Compliance analytics
entities.about:
Compliance tooling cited in eco maps

Landscape role: Compliance analytics. Infrastructure that helps builders and users access Robinhood Chain (RPC, oracles, bridges, compliance, security).

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Infra presence improves UX; it does not remove protocol or bridge risk for end users.

entities.name: Blockaid
entities.notes: Day-1 security partner; free builder tier claim
entities.summary: Security / threat-detection partner with day-1 RH Chain narrative and builder tooling access claims.
entities.about:
Useful as ecosystem security infrastructure — not a substitute for user diligence or audits of individual apps.

Landscape role: Scam/threat detection. Infrastructure that helps builders and users access Robinhood Chain (RPC, oracles, bridges, compliance, security).

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Detection tools reduce some scam surface; they do not eliminate protocol risk.

entities.name: Trust Wallet
entities.notes: Third-party wallet with RH Chain swap promo windows
entities.summary: Third-party self-custody wallet with RH Chain swap promo windows.
entities.about:
Alternative access path to the chain outside Robinhood Wallet.

Promo fee periods are time-boxed — date every claim.
entities.risks: Third-party wallet security model is independent of RH.

entities.name: Uniswap
entities.notes: V2/V3/V4 (+ UniswapX narrative) primary public AMM
entities.summary: Primary public AMM on Robinhood Chain (v2/v3/v4 narrative) — main open liquidity venue by volume/TVL.
entities.about:
Uniswap deployed as the day-1 public liquidity layer for the chain’s open AMM job.

Early RH volume spikes on Uniswap can be meme- and incentive-heavy; separate that from stock-token depth.

Public AMM depth is a different liquidity job than prop venues (Rialto/Pleiades).
entities.risks: Pool liquidity and fee tiers vary wildly by pair; volume ≠ sustainable fees for LPs.

entities.name: Rialto
entities.notes: Prop AMM / spot venue; newsroom day-1 trading list
entities.summary: Prop / specialized spot venue named in official day-1 trading lists alongside Uniswap.
entities.about:
Newsroom and ecosystem copy place Rialto in the onchain spot path for crypto and tokenized assets.

Prop AMM / specialized venues solve a different job than public Uniswap pools — inventory and pricing can differ.

Worth separating public AMM vs prop venue when analyzing “liquidity on RH.”
entities.risks: Venue design and access may differ from permissionless public AMM UX.

entities.name: Pleiades
entities.notes: Proprietary AMM / prop trading venue (newsroom day-1)
entities.summary: Proprietary AMM / prop trading venue called out as a day-1 partner in mainnet narrative.
entities.about:
Positioned as the prop side of the day-1 liquidity story vs Uniswap’s public AMM.

Corporate chain launches often ship both open and controlled liquidity jobs — do not merge them into one “DEX blob.”
entities.risks: Less public surface than Uniswap; verify access and assumptions before comparing volume.

entities.name: 1inch
entities.notes: Router / aggregator in wallet trading narrative
entities.summary: DEX aggregator / router in wallet trading narratives for RH Chain.
entities.about:
Aggregation improves UX by stitching routes; it packages path risk rather than deleting it.

Native single-venue swaps can still be cleaner when liquidity is already where you need it.
entities.risks: Route quality and allowance surfaces still require user caution.

entities.name: 0x
entities.notes: Aggregator volume visible on Llama DEX overview
entities.summary: Swap infrastructure / aggregation layer with volume visible on RH DEX overviews.
entities.about:
Infrastructure for filling swaps across sources — part of the routing stack, not a destination “app brand” for end users.

Landscape role: Swap infrastructure / aggregation. Onchain financial application or venue on Robinhood Chain.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: As with any router, verify allowances and counterparties.

entities.name: Morpho
entities.notes: Largest RH TVL; powers Robinhood Earn rails
entities.summary: Lending backbone on RH Chain; largest TVL slice and rails behind Robinhood Earn narrative.
entities.about:
Morpho Blue-class markets dominate early DeFi TVL on the chain.

Earn funnels attention to USDG lending, but Morpho also represents general onchain credit infrastructure.

Read markets, oracles, and curator setup before treating TVL as “safe yield.”
entities.risks: Smart contract, oracle, and liquidity risks; APY is not guaranteed.

entities.name: Spark
entities.notes: Spark Savings + liquidity layer on RH
entities.summary: Yield / savings style protocols on RH (Spark Savings and related liquidity layer).
entities.about:
Appears in Earn-adjacent and yield job lists; complements Morpho rather than replacing it.

Landscape role: Yield / savings jobs. Onchain financial application or venue on Robinhood Chain.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Yield products can change parameters; read market docs.

entities.name: Ethena
entities.summary: Yield / stable strategy partner named in Robinhood Earn launch narrative.
entities.about:
Part of the curator/strategy stack around USDG lending stories — verify actual deployments on RH before assuming exposure.

Landscape role: Yield / stable strategy partner. Onchain financial application or venue on Robinhood Chain.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Strategy risk is separate from chain and Morpho market risk.

entities.name: Steakhouse
entities.summary: Vault curator partner referenced in Earn / Morpho ecosystem narrative.
entities.about:
Curators shape risk parameters and market design; “partner” status is not a performance guarantee.

Landscape role: Vault curator partner. Onchain financial application or venue on Robinhood Chain.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Curator choices affect borrower/lender outcomes.

entities.name: Maple
entities.summary: Credit / lending partner named in Earn stack narrative.
entities.about:
Institutional-style credit narratives differ from open Morpho markets — confirm what is actually live on RH Chain.

Landscape role: Credit / lending partner. Onchain financial application or venue on Robinhood Chain.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Credit products have default and liquidity risk.

entities.name: Arcus
entities.notes: Perps/spot; ex-dYdX team narrative; high TVL
entities.summary: Perps / derivatives venue on RH Chain with material TVL in early Llama rankings.
entities.about:
Cited as part of the trading stack for tokenized equities and crypto; team narrative often references ex-dYdX builders.

Perps are a different risk surface than spot AMM or stock-token transfer.
entities.risks: Leverage, liquidation, and oracle design — higher complexity than spot swaps.

entities.name: Lighter
entities.summary: ZK-powered perps/spot venue with wallet integration claims on RH Chain.
entities.about:
Part of the derivatives and trading surface beyond pure Uniswap spot flow.

Treat “integrated in wallet” marketing as distribution, not a risk free pass.
entities.risks: Derivatives risk + new-chain operational risk.

entities.name: Curve
entities.summary: Curve-style specialized AMM presence on Robinhood Chain.
entities.about:
Curve is known for efficient stable and volatile pools on other networks; on RH it appears as a smaller DEX TVL slice.

Public AMM depth here is still secondary to Uniswap in early metrics.

Useful as a diversification of pool types, not as the main volume venue.
entities.risks: Pool risk and low early depth — check actual pair liquidity.

entities.name: PancakeSwap
entities.summary: Multi-chain DEX with AMM deployments registered on Robinhood Chain.
entities.about:
PancakeSwap extends a familiar multi-chain DEX brand onto RH.

Early TVL/volume is modest versus Uniswap’s day-1 footprint.

Treat as alternate public swap venue, not the primary liquidity map.
entities.risks: Cross-chain brand ≠ identical risk or liquidity on every deployment.

entities.name: SwapHood
entities.summary: Native RH DEX (V2/V3) — early local swap venue.
entities.about:
SwapHood is a chain-native public AMM rather than a multi-chain bluechip deploy.

Useful for understanding native builder surface; depth may be thin versus Uniswap.

Compare fees, pools, and routing before size.
entities.risks: Early native venue — smart contract and liquidity risk.

entities.name: Sheriff Exchange
entities.summary: Native DEX
entities.about:
Small DEX TVL V2/V3

Landscape role: Native DEX. Onchain financial application or venue on Robinhood Chain.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Smart contract, oracle, and liquidity risks apply. NFA — verify before using.

entities.name: RobinSwap
entities.summary: Native DEX
entities.about:

Landscape role: Native DEX. Onchain financial application or venue on Robinhood Chain.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Smart contract, oracle, and liquidity risks apply. NFA — verify before using.

entities.risks: Smart contract, oracle, and liquidity risks apply. NFA — verify before using.

entities.name: Hoodbets
entities.summary: Prediction markets
entities.about:
Prediction market (small TVL)

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Smart contract, oracle, and liquidity risks apply. NFA — verify before using.

entities.name: DexFi Aggregator
entities.summary: Yield aggregation
entities.about:
Yield aggregator on RH

Landscape role: Yield aggregation. Onchain financial application or venue on Robinhood Chain.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Smart contract, oracle, and liquidity risks apply. NFA — verify before using.

entities.name: Symbiosis
entities.summary: Cross-chain bridge with TVL registered on RH Chain in Llama.
entities.about:
Bridges move assets; they concentrate historical exploit risk in crypto.

Self-custodial bridge UX ≠ riskless path.
entities.risks: Bridge smart contract and liquidity risk.

entities.name: Index Fi
entities.summary: Tokenized index product
entities.about:
Programmatic stock index claims (CT)

Landscape role: Tokenized index product. Onchain financial application or venue on Robinhood Chain.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Smart contract, oracle, and liquidity risks apply. NFA — verify before using.

entities.name: Midas RWA
entities.summary: Tokenized credit / RWA product
entities.about:
mGLO day-1 product claims (CT)

Landscape role: Tokenized credit / RWA product. Onchain financial application or venue on Robinhood Chain.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Smart contract, oracle, and liquidity risks apply. NFA — verify before using.

entities.name: NOXA Fun
entities.summary: Early dominant launchpad rail on RH Chain by community metrics and Llama TVL.
entities.about:
Launchpads explain a large share of permissionless token creation and fee print.

Useful for understanding volume composition — not for “which launch to buy.”
entities.risks: Launchpad UX can hide approval and contract risks; DYOR on contracts.

entities.name: Pons
entities.notes: High deploy count narrative; approval-risk CT notes
entities.summary: High-deploy launchpad rail in the RH “launchpad war”; watch approval UX carefully.
entities.about:
CT tracked deploy leadership flipping among Noxa/Pons/Flap-class apps.

Community posts have flagged approval-surface concerns on related paths — treat as risk case studies and verify contracts yourself.
entities.risks: Unlimited approvals and multicall patterns can be dangerous; revoke unused allowances.

entities.name: Flap
entities.notes: Launchpad war; DEX volume on Llama
entities.summary: Launchpad rail in the RH deploy race; shows meaningful DEX volume in some overviews.
entities.about:
Flap competes with Noxa/Pons-class launchpads for permissionless token creation.

Launchpad volume can dominate chain fee/volume screenshots without proving RWA thesis.

Orientation: mechanisms and competition, not which pad to farm.
entities.risks: Launchpad approval and contract risk; DYOR.

entities.name: 4.fun
entities.notes: No creation fees / no bonding curves claim
entities.summary: Meme launch rail with no-creation-fee / no-bonding-curve marketing claims.
entities.about:
4.fun positions itself as a lighter launch UX on RH Chain.

Such rails amplify attention-layer tokens quickly.

Read actual docs and contracts — marketing claims change.
entities.risks: Meme launch risk + contract risk.

entities.summary: Launchpad (RWA-themed)
entities.about:
RWA-dividend launch narrative

Landscape role: Launchpad (RWA-themed). Permissionless token launch rail — useful for understanding deploy volume, not as investment advice.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Launch rails concentrate contract and approval risk; verify before interacting.

entities.name: Virtuals
entities.notes: Agent launch platform cited in eco maps
entities.summary: Agent launch platform frequently cited in RH Chain agentic / AI layer maps.
entities.about:
Agents are a core official narrative (“AI-native” chain). Virtuals-class platforms are where much of the agent token surface appears.

Agent volume is not the same as RWA product-market fit.
entities.risks: Agent tokens are often highly speculative; treat as noise/attention unless proven utility.

entities.name: RobiNode
entities.summary: Builder focusing on agent routing for tokenized stocks — “listed ≠ liquid” in practice.
entities.about:
Useful peer example: multi-protocol routing with sellability checks for stock tokens.

Highlights that brochure listings and actual fillable routes are different products.
entities.risks: Early builder software; verify before using with size.

entities.name: SLVR
entities.notes: Gamified mining; fee print on Llama
entities.summary: Gamified / attention app
entities.about:
Gamified mining; fee print on Llama

Landscape role: Gamified / attention app. Attention / meme / gamified sample in the early landscape — not complete long-tail coverage.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Highly speculative attention asset; not a research endorsement.

entities.name: Cash Cat
entities.summary: High-visibility native meme on RH Chain — attention layer sample.
entities.about:
Memes prove distribution and low-friction trading; they do not prove stock-token PMF.

Included so the map shows the volume composition story honestly.
entities.risks: Extreme speculation; can go to zero. Not a recommendation.

entities.name: HOODZ
entities.summary: Gaming / attention
entities.about:
Gaming project small TVL

Landscape role: Gaming / attention. Attention / meme / gamified sample in the early landscape — not complete long-tail coverage.

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Highly speculative attention asset; not a research endorsement.

entities.name: News For Robinhood
entities.summary: Independent ecosystem intel
entities.about:
Independent RH intel / wallet clusters

Landscape role: Independent ecosystem intel. Research or data voice useful for orientation (usually secondary unless primary-sourced).

Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
entities.risks: Secondary commentary can be wrong or late — prefer primary sources.

entities.name: The DeFi Edge
entities.notes: Research; memes vs RWA fork framing
entities.summary: Mid-tier research/curation account that framed RH’s memes-vs-RWA fork clearly.
entities.about:
Useful for secondary landscape framing and reading lists.

Still secondary to official docs and onchain data.
entities.risks: Editorial takes can be incomplete — verify numbers.

entities.name: Tom Wan
